---
title: 'Distortion Critique 2'
date: '2016-09-18T04:49:00.0010000+00:00'
draft: true
blogger_blog: 'Darrell Wolfe, Storyteller'
blogger_status: 'LIVE'
blogger_post_id: '101741299301060728'
blogger_filename: '/2016/09/distortion-critique-2_17.html'
blogger_source: 'takeout-20260223T235336Z-3-001.zip:Takeout/Blogger/Blogs/Darrell Wolfe, Storyteller/feed.atom'
categories:
  - 'critique'
  - 'White Noise'
---

<br /><br />So I put out scene one from Distortion for a writer's critique. Here was the result:<br /><br /><br /><br />It's amazing how awesome this feedback was. You can live with your stuff long enough that you become blind to it. I have a lot of work to do (first novel) but I've studied the craft long enough to understand everything everyone is saying. Besides all the specific points, the two themes I noticed were a lack of emotional/physical reaction (nothing to keep you in Ash's head) and the Great Sin (show don't tell). I also changed tense and didn't notice (no that was not on purpose). There were many other great points, and I have a lot of work to do. Thanks again to <a href="http://www.scribophile.com/" target="_blank">http://www.scribophile.com/ </a>for making a community for writers.<br /><br /><br /><br /><div class="user" style="background-color: white; color: #222222; font-family: Verdana, sans-serif; font-size: 14px; position: relative; z-index: 10;"><br /><div style="font-weight: bold; text-align: center; word-wrap: break-word;"><br /><a href="http://www.scribophile.com/authors/devi-thomas/" style="color: #c70c14; outline: 0px; text-decoration: none;">Devi Thomas</a></div><br /></div><br /><div class="bubble" style="background-color: white; border-radius: 8px; border: 2px solid rgb(221, 221, 221); color: #222222; font-family: Verdana, sans-serif; font-size: 14px; margin-left: 90px; position: relative;"><br /><div class="comment" style="padding: 10px;"><br /><h4 style="border-bottom: 2px solid rgb(238, 238, 238); color: #353e34; font-size: 16px; line-height: 22px; margin: 0px 0px 10px; padding-bottom: 5px;"><br />Opening Comments</h4><br /><div style="margin-top: 10px;"><br />critiquing this based on the cool name and nothing else... gonna comment as I go...</div><br /><h4 style="border-bottom: 2px solid rgb(238, 238, 238); color: #353e34; font-size: 16px; line-height: 22px; margin: 40px 0px 10px; padding-bottom: 5px;"><br />Inline Critique</h4><br /><div class="inline-comment-body" id="inline-comment-651323"><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important;"><br />Out of the corner of his eye, through the window of The Grounded Cafe, Ash saw two men dressed in black combat gear duck behind a car.</div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /><mark style="-webkit-print-color-adjust: exact; background-color: #fff293; color: #111111; font-style: inherit !important; font-weight: inherit !important; line-height: 26px !important;">Coolness&nbsp;</mark><br /><br /><ins style="-webkit-print-color-adjust: exact; background-color: #98e187; color: #111111; font-family: Verdana, sans-serif !important; font-size: 14px !important; line-height: 26px !important; padding: 0px 5px; text-decoration: none !important; white-space: pre-wrap;">Or you could say, 'A chill' or 'ice' or my spine became a stalactite... okay that my be overdoing it, I guess.  But to me coolness is usually a good thing.  Like, people are cool.  Plus coolness is kind of 'tepid'.  It doesn't seem harsh enough.</ins>rushed down his spine, and his muscles tensed.</div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br />He reached for his sidearm.</div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br />He shook his head, and looked again, they were gone.</div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br />Ash relaxed and sank back into the leather bound chair; alone, in the corner of his favorite coffee shop.</div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br />Always keep your back to the wall, be near the most exits, but never too visible. It was like breathing, it never stopped... even in civilian life. Post-Traumatic Stress Disorder, PTSD. That was the diagnosis they’d assigned him.<br /><br /><ins style="-webkit-print-color-adjust: exact; background-color: #98e187; color: #111111; font-family: Verdana, sans-serif !important; font-size: 14px !important; line-height: 26px !important; padding: 0px 5px; text-decoration: none !important; white-space: pre-wrap;">Oh this is great.  Love it.</ins></div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br />As Dean of Military Science, Ash's job primarily lay in administrative work and policy these days, plus a few sessions counseling the younger cadets, and would-be trouble makers. He was&nbsp;<mark style="-webkit-print-color-adjust: exact; background-color: #fff293; color: #111111; font-style: inherit !important; font-weight: inherit !important; line-height: 26px !important;">quite</mark><br /><br /><ins style="-webkit-print-color-adjust: exact; background-color: #98e187; color: #111111; font-family: Verdana, sans-serif !important; font-size: 14px !important; line-height: 26px !important; padding: 0px 5px; text-decoration: none !important; white-space: pre-wrap;">again this seems a bit tepid.  Either cut it or commit to it.  'extremely' overqulified might have more impact.</ins>&nbsp;overqualified for the position, but he ran a tight unit.</div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br />He stared at the faces of the pedestrians as they passed by the window. Hurried. Busy. Buried in their smartphones.<br /><br /><ins style="-webkit-print-color-adjust: exact; background-color: #98e187; color: #111111; font-family: Verdana, sans-serif !important; font-size: 14px !important; line-height: 26px !important; padding: 0px 5px; text-decoration: none !important; white-space: pre-wrap;">oh yes aren't they always.</ins>So oblivious to the world around them, or the little clouds that float around their shoulders.<br /><br /><ins style="-webkit-print-color-adjust: exact; background-color: #98e187; color: #111111; font-family: Verdana, sans-serif !important; font-size: 14px !important; line-height: 26px !important; padding: 0px 5px; text-decoration: none !important; white-space: pre-wrap;">this is awesome.</ins></div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /><del style="-webkit-print-color-adjust: exact; background-color: #ec6f6f; color: #111111; font-style: inherit !important; font-weight: inherit !important; line-height: 26px !important;">The&nbsp;</del>leaves<br /><br /><ins style="-webkit-print-color-adjust: exact; background-color: #98e187; color: #111111; font-family: Verdana, sans-serif !important; font-size: 14px !important; line-height: 26px !important; padding: 0px 5px; text-decoration: none !important; white-space: pre-wrap;">fell</ins><br /><br /><del style="-webkit-print-color-adjust: exact; background-color: #ec6f6f; color: #111111; font-style: inherit !important; font-weight: inherit !important; line-height: 26px !important;">began to fall,</del>&nbsp;most turning shades of orange and yellow, matching perfectly the Pumpkins that were dotting porches and windows through the city<br /><br /><del style="-webkit-print-color-adjust: exact; background-color: #ec6f6f; color: #111111; font-style: inherit !important; font-weight: inherit !important; line-height: 26px !important;">the past few weeks</del>.</div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br />Some commotion outside broke his train of thought.<br /><br /><del style="-webkit-print-color-adjust: exact; background-color: #ec6f6f; color: #111111; font-style: inherit !important; font-weight: inherit !important; line-height: 26px !important;">..</del>&nbsp;Everyone was stopping, turning, and staring in the same direction, a few moving toward the distraction.</div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br />Ash stepped outside to see what was happening, turned a corner, and nearly ran into a bystander.&nbsp;<mark style="-webkit-print-color-adjust: exact; background-color: #fff293; color: #111111; font-style: inherit !important; font-weight: inherit !important; line-height: 26px !important;">A brisk evening breeze broke past his collar.</mark><br /><br /><ins style="-webkit-print-color-adjust: exact; background-color: #98e187; color: #111111; font-family: Verdana, sans-serif !important; font-size: 14px !important; line-height: 26px !important; padding: 0px 5px; text-decoration: none !important; white-space: pre-wrap;">is this relevant?</ins></div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br />A man in a tattered and dirty suit with a graying beard was standing on top of a pickup truck screaming at nobody in particular.</div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br />"You always did love Mother best... I know... That's why... You couldn't keep your mouth shut..." On he went, incoherently.</div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br />Just as Ash took a step closer, the man turned to stare him directly in the eye.</div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br />In a deeper, gravelly voice: "You... What do you want with us?"</div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br />Ash took a step back.</div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br />Then the man turned back to his one-sided conversation.</div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /><br /><br /><ins style="-webkit-print-color-adjust: exact; background-color: #98e187; color: #111111; font-family: Verdana, sans-serif !important; font-size: 14px !important; line-height: 26px !important; padding: 0px 5px; text-decoration: none !important; white-space: pre-wrap;">creepy!</ins></div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /><del style="-webkit-print-color-adjust: exact; background-color: #ec6f6f; color: #111111; font-style: inherit !important; font-weight: inherit !important; line-height: 26px !important;">Ash could see&nbsp;</del><ins style="-webkit-print-color-adjust: exact; background-color: #98e187; color: #111111; font-family: Verdana, sans-serif !important; font-size: 14px !important; line-height: 26px !important; padding: 0px 5px; text-decoration: none !important; white-space: pre-wrap;">There was</ins>a large creature<br /><br /><ins style="-webkit-print-color-adjust: exact; background-color: #98e187; color: #111111; font-family: Verdana, sans-serif !important; font-size: 14px !important; line-height: 26px !important; padding: 0px 5px; text-decoration: none !important; white-space: pre-wrap;">appeared</ins>behind the man. The bottom half was more mist than form, as though it were made of smoke. The top half was like a man, with the head of a bull. It was charcoal,<br /><br /><del style="-webkit-print-color-adjust: exact; background-color: #ec6f6f; color: #111111; font-style: inherit !important; font-weight: inherit !important; line-height: 26px !important;">and</del><br /><br /><ins style="-webkit-print-color-adjust: exact; background-color: #98e187; color: #111111; font-family: Verdana, sans-serif !important; font-size: 14px !important; line-height: 26px !important; padding: 0px 5px; text-decoration: none !important; white-space: pre-wrap;">with</ins>streaks of white painted across the chest.<br /><br /><ins style="-webkit-print-color-adjust: exact; background-color: #98e187; color: #111111; font-family: Verdana, sans-serif !important; font-size: 14px !important; line-height: 26px !important; padding: 0px 5px; text-decoration: none !important; white-space: pre-wrap;">I dded appeared here because at first Ash seems to think the man is having a one-sided conversation.  But if he w=saw these creatures right away, then he'd never had reacted as if he thought the man was having a one-sides convo.  The issue here is, you seem to have described the way everyone else is seeing the situation but you've done it from Ash's perspective.  So either he needs to see the creatures right away or they need to gradually - or suddenly appear.</ins></div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br />Ash<br /><br /><ins style="-webkit-print-color-adjust: exact; background-color: #98e187; color: #111111; font-family: Verdana, sans-serif !important; font-size: 14px !important; line-height: 26px !important; padding: 0px 5px; text-decoration: none !important; white-space: pre-wrap;">'s</ins><del style="-webkit-print-color-adjust: exact; background-color: #ec6f6f; color: #111111; font-style: inherit !important; font-weight: inherit !important; line-height: 26px !important;">&nbsp;could feel his</del>&nbsp;palms turn cold,<br /><br /><del style="-webkit-print-color-adjust: exact; background-color: #ec6f6f; color: #111111; font-style: inherit !important; font-weight: inherit !important; line-height: 26px !important;">and he felt as though</del><ins style="-webkit-print-color-adjust: exact; background-color: #98e187; color: #111111; font-family: Verdana, sans-serif !important; font-size: 14px !important; line-height: 26px !important; padding: 0px 5px; text-decoration: none !important; white-space: pre-wrap;">and</ins>&nbsp;he couldn't move his legs.<br /><br /><ins style="-webkit-print-color-adjust: exact; background-color: #98e187; color: #111111; font-family: Verdana, sans-serif !important; font-size: 14px !important; line-height: 26px !important; padding: 0px 5px; text-decoration: none !important; white-space: pre-wrap;">I feel there should be a more extreme reaction here from Ash.  he's seeing this wholly unbelievable thing right now and he's not freakling out at all. At least, you aren't showing us he's freaking out.  This would be a good place for some inner monologue, or maybe some swearing under his breath or something.  I wat to know how he's feeling right now.</ins></div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br />A smaller creature was on the homeless man's shoulders, screaming into his ears. He couldn't hear the conversation, but Ash could tell that the homeless man was responding and that he had only been hearing one-half of the conversation. Ash looked around and nobody else seemed too surprised, as usual.</div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br />The large creature stared directly at Ash, cocked his head to one side and<mark style="-webkit-print-color-adjust: exact; background-color: #fff293; color: #111111; font-style: inherit !important; font-weight: inherit !important; line-height: 26px !important;">disappeared along with the smaller one</mark>.<br /><br /><ins style="-webkit-print-color-adjust: exact; background-color: #98e187; color: #111111; font-family: Verdana, sans-serif !important; font-size: 14px !important; line-height: 26px !important; padding: 0px 5px; text-decoration: none !important; white-space: pre-wrap;">Confused.  Where did he disappear?</ins>The homeless man was by himself again, then his eyes narrowed. He leapt off the truck and ran at Ash.</div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br />It took all his training to keep from being pinned down. The old man was wiry but stronger than he looked.</div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br />Ash let out a cry<br /><br /><ins style="-webkit-print-color-adjust: exact; background-color: #98e187; color: #111111; font-family: Verdana, sans-serif !important; font-size: 14px !important; line-height: 26px !important; padding: 0px 5px; text-decoration: none !important; white-space: pre-wrap;">.</ins>, "Jesus!"</div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br />The wiry man stepped back, his eyes darted around, as though he looked confused.</div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br />That was all Ash needed. In 30 seconds he hog-tied the man with his own belt.</div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br />The police were just arriving. "Charlie's at it again... Let's take him in."</div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br />Three big men in uniform put cuffs and a muzzle on the old man, and threw him,<br /><br /><del style="-webkit-print-color-adjust: exact; background-color: #ec6f6f; color: #111111; font-style: inherit !important; font-weight: inherit !important; line-height: 26px !important;">rathe</del>r harshly, into the back of the squad car.</div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br />Ash heard someone behind him<br /><br /><ins style="-webkit-print-color-adjust: exact; background-color: #98e187; color: #111111; font-family: Verdana, sans-serif !important; font-size: 14px !important; line-height: 26px !important; padding: 0px 5px; text-decoration: none !important; white-space: pre-wrap;">.</ins>, "Those were some moves, Marine."</div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br />"Airman. Uh... Thanks. No harm done." Ash noticed his cup crushed beneath him, the ice, coffee, and cream covered the sidewalk, and his jeans. "Well... Almost no harm anyway."<br /><br /><ins style="-webkit-print-color-adjust: exact; background-color: #98e187; color: #111111; font-family: Verdana, sans-serif !important; font-size: 14px !important; line-height: 26px !important; padding: 0px 5px; text-decoration: none !important; white-space: pre-wrap;">ha ha.  I dunno. I'd be pretty grumpy to lose my coffee like that.</ins></div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br />"Why don't you let me get you another… on me."</div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br />Ash looked up to see a balding man in blue slacks, yellow polo, and a pile of papers in his arms. A logo with two E’s overlapping bore prominently on his chest.</div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br />"The least I could do for a man who'd be willing to step into a situation like that, protect all these people. What's your name son?"</div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br />"Ash. I need to get some new clothes. I don't think I need any more caffeine right now. But thanks for the offer." With a wave, he set off toward home.</div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br />Ash headed down the sidewalk, past the shops and restaurants. There<br /><br /><del style="-webkit-print-color-adjust: exact; background-color: #ec6f6f; color: #111111; font-style: inherit !important; font-weight: inherit !important; line-height: 26px !important;">is</del><br /><br /><ins style="-webkit-print-color-adjust: exact; background-color: #98e187; color: #111111; font-family: Verdana, sans-serif !important; font-size: 14px !important; line-height: 26px !important; padding: 0px 5px; text-decoration: none !important; white-space: pre-wrap;">was</ins>a girl crying, typing away on<br /><br /><ins style="-webkit-print-color-adjust: exact; background-color: #98e187; color: #111111; font-family: Verdana, sans-serif !important; font-size: 14px !important; line-height: 26px !important; padding: 0px 5px; text-decoration: none !important; white-space: pre-wrap;">a</ins><del style="-webkit-print-color-adjust: exact; background-color: #ec6f6f; color: #111111; font-style: inherit !important; font-weight: inherit !important; line-height: 26px !important;">the</del>&nbsp;phone. A little red frog on her shoulder was yelling into her ear. Ash could only imagine what it must be saying.</div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br />A little old lady<br /><br /><del style="-webkit-print-color-adjust: exact; background-color: #ec6f6f; color: #111111; font-style: inherit !important; font-weight: inherit !important; line-height: 26px !important;">is</del><br /><br /><ins style="-webkit-print-color-adjust: exact; background-color: #98e187; color: #111111; font-family: Verdana, sans-serif !important; font-size: 14px !important; line-height: 26px !important; padding: 0px 5px; text-decoration: none !important; white-space: pre-wrap;">was</ins>sitting inside a window at the nursing home. She is<br /><br /><ins style="-webkit-print-color-adjust: exact; background-color: #98e187; color: #111111; font-family: Verdana, sans-serif !important; font-size: 14px !important; line-height: 26px !important; padding: 0px 5px; text-decoration: none !important; white-space: pre-wrap;">was</ins>knitting something, probably a blanket. A large man in all white, stands<br /><br /><ins style="-webkit-print-color-adjust: exact; background-color: #98e187; color: #111111; font-family: Verdana, sans-serif !important; font-size: 14px !important; line-height: 26px !important; padding: 0px 5px; text-decoration: none !important; white-space: pre-wrap;">stood</ins>behind her, smiling. He looks<br /><br /><ins style="-webkit-print-color-adjust: exact; background-color: #98e187; color: #111111; font-family: Verdana, sans-serif !important; font-size: 14px !important; line-height: 26px !important; padding: 0px 5px; text-decoration: none !important; white-space: pre-wrap;">looked</ins>&nbsp;up at Ash, his smile fades<br /><br /><ins style="-webkit-print-color-adjust: exact; background-color: #98e187; color: #111111; font-family: Verdana, sans-serif !important; font-size: 14px !important; line-height: 26px !important; padding: 0px 5px; text-decoration: none !important; white-space: pre-wrap;">faded</ins>&nbsp;slightly, and then<br /><br /><ins style="-webkit-print-color-adjust: exact; background-color: #98e187; color: #111111; font-family: Verdana, sans-serif !important; font-size: 14px !important; line-height: 26px !important; padding: 0px 5px; text-decoration: none !important; white-space: pre-wrap;">returned</ins>returns. He looks<br /><br /><ins style="-webkit-print-color-adjust: exact; background-color: #98e187; color: #111111; font-family: Verdana, sans-serif !important; font-size: 14px !important; line-height: 26px !important; padding: 0px 5px; text-decoration: none !important; white-space: pre-wrap;">looked</ins>&nbsp;down to her again.<br /><br /><ins style="-webkit-print-color-adjust: exact; background-color: #98e187; color: #111111; font-family: Verdana, sans-serif !important; font-size: 14px !important; line-height: 26px !important; padding: 0px 5px; text-decoration: none !important; white-space: pre-wrap;">you've changed tense.  That's a shame.  It was going so well.&nbsp;unless its purposeful for some reason i'm missing.  if so, I don't think it works.</ins></div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br />Ash could not see the figure accompanying each person, only certain ones, here and there. Most were just clouds or mist.<br /><br /><ins style="-webkit-print-color-adjust: exact; background-color: #98e187; color: #111111; font-family: Verdana, sans-serif !important; font-size: 14px !important; line-height: 26px !important; padding: 0px 5px; text-decoration: none !important; white-space: pre-wrap;">how did he know figures accompanied everyone.  maybe they didn't.</ins></div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br />"Don't worry so much." His doctor told him<br /><br /><ins style="-webkit-print-color-adjust: exact; background-color: #98e187; color: #111111; font-family: Verdana, sans-serif !important; font-size: 14px !important; line-height: 26px !important; padding: 0px 5px; text-decoration: none !important; white-space: pre-wrap;">.</ins>, "These may be after effects of the war. We’re just beginning to understand PTSD. The mind is a marvelous adapter to stress. As long as these phantoms do not interrupt your daily work, or cause you to want to harm yourself or others, you should be fine. Consider yourself lucky. Just think of them as a construct of your active imagination, an amusing distraction from the mundane realities of life."<br /><br /><ins style="-webkit-print-color-adjust: exact; background-color: #98e187; color: #111111; font-family: Verdana, sans-serif !important; font-size: 14px !important; line-height: 26px !important; padding: 0px 5px; text-decoration: none !important; white-space: pre-wrap;">ah, so this isn't a new thing.  This has been going on a long time.  Cool.  No wonder he wasn't freaking out and shocked.  You might need to get a line or two in early on, saying something like, 'Ash had learned long ago to stifle his reactions of such creatures'... or something much more tactfully worded than my iffy skills allow.</ins></div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br />He turned off the street into an alley between two old buildings, and up a well-worn path into the woods, his refuge from the masses.<br /><br /><ins style="-webkit-print-color-adjust: exact; background-color: #98e187; color: #111111; font-family: Verdana, sans-serif !important; font-size: 14px !important; line-height: 26px !important; padding: 0px 5px; text-decoration: none !important; white-space: pre-wrap;">nice</ins></div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br />Ash opened the door of his cabin.</div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br />It wasn't a mansion by any means, but it was more than sufficient for him. 900 sq. ft., one large open room, a loft, and a balcony.</div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br />Yes sir, he owned one fine cabin in the woods.<br /><br /><ins style="-webkit-print-color-adjust: exact; background-color: #98e187; color: #111111; font-family: Verdana, sans-serif !important; font-size: 14px !important; line-height: 26px !important; padding: 0px 5px; text-decoration: none !important; white-space: pre-wrap;">cabin in the woods.  Oh this won't end badly at all.  Ha ha</ins>All to himself. How he'd managed to live here eight years and still not have a single friend invited over was a mystery, even to himself. Then again, you'd have to have a friend to invite one.</div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br />He liked things just so. He liked the solitude.</div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br />At least that's what he told himself.<br /><br /><ins style="-webkit-print-color-adjust: exact; background-color: #98e187; color: #111111; font-family: Verdana, sans-serif !important; font-size: 14px !important; line-height: 26px !important; padding: 0px 5px; text-decoration: none !important; white-space: pre-wrap;">nice</ins></div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br />Ash took a seat on his balcony, overlooking the hills and lake. He stared at the expansive forests, lost in time. The clock read: 6:00pm, he laid down in his hammock, bundled in the overpriced winter resistant sleeping bag, and watched the stars from the balcony until he drifted off.</div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br />As sleep overtook him, he thought to himself<br /><br /><ins style="-webkit-print-color-adjust: exact; background-color: #98e187; color: #111111; font-family: Verdana, sans-serif !important; font-size: 14px !important; line-height: 26px !important; padding: 0px 5px; text-decoration: none !important; white-space: pre-wrap;">,</ins>&nbsp;"Yes sir, this is one fine cabin."</div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br />***</div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br />An explosion knocks him against a wall. He barely sees, through the smoke, troops running about, hauling rubble off of bodies, checking pulses. Some are screaming in pain. Others shouting to one another<br /><br /><ins style="-webkit-print-color-adjust: exact; background-color: #98e187; color: #111111; font-family: Verdana, sans-serif !important; font-size: 14px !important; line-height: 26px !important; padding: 0px 5px; text-decoration: none !important; white-space: pre-wrap;">in fear</ins>. Half the mess hall is completely gone, all that is left is fire and rubble.</div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br />Ash can feel the beat of his heart in his neck, his forehead&nbsp;<mark style="-webkit-print-color-adjust: exact; background-color: #fff293; color: #111111; font-style: inherit !important; font-weight: inherit !important; line-height: 26px !important;">rushed</mark><br /><br /><ins style="-webkit-print-color-adjust: exact; background-color: #98e187; color: #111111; font-family: Verdana, sans-serif !important; font-size: 14px !important; line-height: 26px !important; padding: 0px 5px; text-decoration: none !important; white-space: pre-wrap;">are you mising up tenses again?  should this be rushes?</ins>with sweat. He sees them all running around, but all he hears is intense ringing.</div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br />As the numbness subsides from his brain, shock waves hit his chest as more buildings are getting hit nearby.</div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br />Ash uses the wall to steady himself and works to stand on his feet.</div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br />Others will tend to the wounded. This band of Jihadist miscreants found upgraded weapons; Russian no doubt.<br /><br /><ins style="-webkit-print-color-adjust: exact; background-color: #98e187; color: #111111; font-family: Verdana, sans-serif !important; font-size: 14px !important; line-height: 26px !important; padding: 0px 5px; text-decoration: none !important; white-space: pre-wrap;">hmm.  i liked it better when the enemy was unnamed.  I strongly feel that unless you are writing a piece about a particular war, leave naming the enemy out of it.  I feel this is unnecessary and encourages hate and spreads fear.&nbsp;As far as I can see, this story is about the effects of war.  Not the actual war.  If that's the case, is it necessary to name the enemy?</ins></div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br />Ash shoots out the building, down the road to the artillery unit. He grabs his rifle and heads for the highest structure, a radio tower toward the side of the camp.</div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br />He can see the launch area where the rockets are being fired from. He takes a few quick breaths, hyperventilating floods the lungs with oxygen, one last deep breath in and hold, steadies his body.</div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br />Through the scope, he can see about 10 of them.</div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br />They only have two missile launchers. They have very little cover, firing from open desert, mostly hiding behind their Jeep and a few large outcroppings of fallen mountainside.</div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br />He fires two rounds back to back, taking out both missile launchers, one exploding in the handlers face. Ash then begins firing one round after another as the attackers fall like Coke cans on a fence post. The rounds hit their targets with extreme prejudice.</div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br />Three remain, cowering behind the Jeep. Jeeps are great vehicles for driving through tough terrain quickly, but they are built light, not armored. He sees one peek through the glass, gone.</div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br />The other two attempt to climb in the vehicle keeping their heads down and drive away. The problem with that is that both seats are occupied. He doesn't need to see them. Two. One.</div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br />The Jeep speeds up as it drives erratically, then crashes into a mound of rocks.</div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br />***</div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br />Ash woke up... another nightmare, if you can call a memory a nightmare.</div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br />He rubbed his eyes and glanced at his wrist.</div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /><del style="-webkit-print-color-adjust: exact; background-color: #ec6f6f; color: #111111; font-style: inherit !important; font-weight: inherit !important; line-height: 26px !important;">Red numbers flooded his vision,&nbsp;</del>3:00 am.</div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br /><br /></div><br /><div style="font-family: Georgia, serif !important; font-size: 16px !important; line-height: 26px !important; margin-top: 10px;"><br />He rolled over and went back to sleep.</div><br /></div><br /><h4 style="border-bottom: 2px solid rgb(238, 238, 238); color: #353e34; font-size: 16px; line-height: 22px; margin: 40px 0px 10px; padding-bottom: 5px;"><br />Closing Comments</h4><br /><div style="margin-top: 10px;"><br />This is a great start. &nbsp;From here it could go anywhere. &nbsp;Will he lose the plot completely and start believing in his monsters? &nbsp;Or will the monsters turn out to be real? Or maybe I'm on the complete wrong track and it will be a great surprise. &nbsp;</div><br /><div style="margin-top: 10px;"><br />The only main issue I saw with this was the occasional tense change. &nbsp;I get the dream part - that works well. &nbsp;But during the other scenes it happens a couple times too. &nbsp;I marked what I saw as a problem. &nbsp;Do with it whatever you wish. &nbsp;</div><br /><div style="margin-top: 10px;"><br />I hope my thoughts and idea are helpful to you and I look forward to reading more.</div><br /></div><br /></div><br /><br /><br /><br /><br /><blockquote class="tr_bq" style="background-color: white; color: #333333; font-family: Verdana, Geneva, sans-serif; font-size: 15.4px; line-height: 19.6px;"><br /><div><br /><br /></div><br /></blockquote><br /><h4 style="background-color: white; color: #333333; font-family: Verdana, Geneva, sans-serif; font-size: 15.4px; line-height: 19.6px; margin: 0px; position: relative;"><br />By&nbsp;<a href="https://plus.google.com/+DarrellWolfe/posts" style="color: #149adf; text-decoration: none;" target="_blank">Darrell Wolfe</a></h4><br /><blockquote class="tr_bq" style="background-color: white; color: #333333; font-family: Verdana, Geneva, sans-serif; font-size: 15.4px; line-height: 19.6px;"><br /><i>Storyteller | Creative | <a href="http://www.16personalities.com/infj-personality" style="color: #149adf; text-decoration: none;" target="_blank">INFJ</a>&nbsp;|&nbsp;<a href="https://www.gallupstrengthscenter.com/Purchase/en-US/Product?Path=Clifton%20StrengthsFinder" style="color: #149adf; text-decoration: none;" target="_blank">Intellection</a>&nbsp;|&nbsp;Ideation | Input | Learner | Achiever | Multipotentialite</i></blockquote><br /><ul><br /><li><i>See my full about page and disclosures: <a href="http://authorfun.blogspot.com/p/what-is-this.html" target="_blank">Click Here</a>.&nbsp;</i></li><br /><li><i>Also check out my <a href="http://authorfun.blogspot.com/p/affiliate-store.html" target="_blank">Resources and Suggestions </a>for writers (<a href="http://authorfun.blogspot.com/p/affiliate-store.html" target="_blank">here</a>).&nbsp;</i></li><br /><li><i>To Subscribe to the <a href="http://feeds.feedburner.com/AuthorFun" target="_blank">RSS: Click Here</a></i></li><br /><li><i>For Non-Fiction writing by <a href="http://www.darrellwolfe.com/" target="_blank">Darrell Wolfe: Click Here</a></i></li><br /></ul><br /><ul><br /></ul><br />
